# FlagFit Pro ‚Äî Source of Truth

**The single authoritative doc.** It replaces the prior ~33 hand-written docs (which drifted; see git history). Rule: where this file or the generated files conflict with old prose, **ground truth wins**. Ground truth, in priority order: live Supabase schema ‚Üí actual routes/handlers ‚Üí service code ‚Üí applied migrations. Prose is not ground truth.

- **Data Model** (generated, exact live names): [`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)
- **Endpoint Reference** (generated): [`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)
- **Live schema snapshot** (the offline ground-truth input): [`docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json`](generated/live-schema.snapshot.json)

Regenerate the generated sections: `npm run docs:regen` (see ¬ß8).
Verified against live: **2026-07-13** (Workstream A close-out ‚Äî 5 legacy exercise tables dropped ‚Äî plus the full tables‚ÜîUI / calc / config audit; via authenticated Supabase MCP) ‚Äî 176 base tables, 7 views, 121 Netlify functions (117 exercised, 4 orphaned), 261 migration files in `supabase/migrations/`. The generated DATA_MODEL/ENDPOINTS, `live-schema.snapshot.json`, and `supabase-types.ts` were all regenerated 2026-07-13 (see ¬ß6).

---

## 1. System Map

**Stack.** Angular 22 (standalone, zoneless, signals; NO PrimeNG ‚Äî the static-first rebuild removed it) ‚Üí built by Netlify ‚Üí Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Realtime + Storage). API is **Netlify Functions** under `netlify/functions/*.js`, exposed at `/api/*` via `netlify.toml` redirects. Functions use the **ESM** `export const handler` signature (not `exports.handler`) and the shared service-role client in `netlify/functions/supabase-client.js`. There is **no `angular/src/app/features/` directory** ‚Äî feature screens are direct children of `angular/src/app/` (today, wellness, training, stats, schedule, competition, roster, settings, onboarding, monitoring-report, ‚Ä¶). Routing: `app/app.routes.ts` + `app/core/routes/feature-routes.ts`.

**Engine.** Readiness and ACWR/load are computed server-side and read-through by the client (Readiness: `netlify/functions/calc-readiness.js` ‚Üí `readiness_scores`; ACWR: `compute-acwr.js` + `utils/acwr.js`, 7d acute / 21d chronic uncoupled EWMA, over `training_sessions`). The daily prescription is computed by a **shared pure engine** (`periodization-engine`, mirrored client/server, proven byte-identical), and the whole **week** is planned by one shared `planWeek()`: `today` = `weekAhead()[0]`, This Week = `weekAhead()`, and the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` (now week-aware) all run the identical `planWeek`, so they cannot drift (2026-07-12). See ¬ß5a and Law #6. Schedule spine: `v_athlete_schedule` (union across team memberships + athlete-entered `athlete_events`).

**Lanes.** `/api/coach-*` and `/api/staff-*` (nutritionist/physiotherapist/psychology) are role-gated staff lanes; most athlete features hit the un-prefixed lanes. Many functions are **routers** that delegate to sub-modules via ESM import (e.g. `social.js` ‚Üí `chat.js`/`community.js`; `training.js` ‚Üí `training-*`); sub-modules have no own `/api` redirect but are reachable through the parent.

**Realtime.** `core/services/realtime.service.ts` is the central `postgres_changes` manager (subscribes `training_sessions`, `daily_wellness_checkin`, `readiness_scores`, `chat_messages`, `channels`, `notifications`, `team_members`, `coach_activity_log`, `games`, `messages`, `performance_metrics`). `acwr.service` also subscribes `training_sessions`; `channel.service` delegates to `realtime.service`. (Realtime only fires for tables in the `supabase_realtime` publication ‚Äî verify before relying on it.)

**Auth.** Supabase Auth, localStorage + Bearer token (NOT cookies; no CSRF tokens). The route guard `core/guards/auth.guard.ts` is **config-gated** by `environment.auth.required` ‚Äî open in dev/smoke, enforcing in prod. Only two guards exist: `auth.guard.ts`, `staff.guard.ts`.

---

## 2. Data Model

Generated, exact live names/columns/nullability, with which endpoints touch each table and a DRIFT flag for live tables lacking a migration file: **[`docs/generated/DATA_MODEL.md`](generated/DATA_MODEL.md)**. 176 base tables, 7 views (`physical_measurements_latest`, `user_achievements`, `v_athlete_schedule`, `v_injuries_unified`, `v_pending_event_participation`, `v_seed_integrity`, `v_training_sessions_consent`). Canonical entity names that have historically been mis-inferred: wellness = **`daily_wellness_checkin`** (not `wellness_checkins`/`wellness_logs`); injuries = **`athlete_injuries`** + `v_injuries_unified` (not `injuries`); load source = **`training_sessions`** (not `workout_logs`/`load_monitoring`).

> `supabase-types.ts` was regenerated from the live schema **2026-07-13** (via Supabase MCP `generate_typescript_types`) ‚Äî 0 stale-in-types, 0 live-missing-from-types. The live snapshot remains the ground truth for the data model; regenerate types again after any schema change.

## 3. Endpoint Reference

Generated, every route with method / `/api` path / tables-and-RPCs touched / EXERCISED|ORPHANED: **[`docs/generated/ENDPOINTS.md`](generated/ENDPOINTS.md)**. 121 functions (117 exercised, 4 orphaned: `auth-reset-password`, `session-load-import`, `team-practice-plan`, `wearable-health-ingest` ‚Äî `session-load-import`/`wearable-health-ingest` are the monitoring-feature device/provider ingest lanes, designed for external callers with no Angular upload UI; `team-practice-plan` is the new coach-facing periodized practice-plan generator, no Angular caller wired yet). `periodization-prescription` (the server-side prescription endpoint, ¬ß5a) is now called by `PeriodizationService` and correctly shows EXERCISED. Orphaned endpoints stay listed so nobody rebuilds them. As of 2026-07-13 **no function references a non-existent table** ‚Äî the last ghost-table `.from()` refs (`daily-training.js` still querying `plyometrics_exercises`/`isometrics_exercises` after Workstream A dropped them) were retired (see ¬ß6). The standalone `exercisedb`/`exercises-core`/`isometrics`/`plyometrics` browse endpoints were **deleted** in the same pass ‚Äî the daily-protocol engine reads the canonical `exercises` table directly.

Added `/api/team-join` + `/api/team-join/*` redirects. The two AUTO-INGEST endpoints (`session-load-import` bulk provider export, `wearable-health-ingest` consumer-device consent+ingest) still have no Angular UI ‚Äî ORPHANED, because real OAuth/device adapters (provider credentials) aren't built; not a bug. ## 5. Spec Laws (durable product rules)

1. **P0 ‚Äî never advance past a failed critical write.** If a write that the next step depends on fails (onboarding profile, check-in, account action), surface the error and stop; do not navigate forward as if it succeeded.
2. **Physio/injury blocks override training prescriptions.** An active `athlete_injuries` / tightness signal takes precedence over the engine's prescribed session ‚Äî the plan works around the flagged region.
3. **Nutrition is food-first.** Athlete-facing nutrition uses food/portion language, not g/kg notation.
4. **Answer-first Today ordering.** The Today screen leads with the day's answer (prescription + readiness), diagnostics below the fold.
5. **Onboarding delivers value before asking for complexity.** Show usefulness early; defer heavy data collection.
6. **Server is canonical for the engine; the day is computed once.** Readiness/ACWR are computed server-side and flow into the engine as inputs (`readAcwr`/`readReadiness` prefer the server values). The daily *prescription* is produced by a **single shared function `planWeek()`** in the parity-tested engine (TS source ‚Üí generated port): the client's `today` (= day 0 of the week plan), `weekAhead()` (This Week), the COMPOSE intent handed to `daily-protocol`, and the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` all run the identical `planWeek`, so they **cannot drift** (2026-07-12 ‚Äî replaces the prior "today prefers a single-day server prescription" model, which skipped the week-level rest-minimum and caused a Today-vs-This-Week drift; ¬ß5a). The server endpoint is now a runtime drift *canary*, not the render source. Missing data ‚Üí explicit empty state, never a fabricated number.
7. **No fabricated UI data.** Inputs prefill from real saved state; never show placeholder values that masquerade as the athlete's data.

### 5a. Recovery modalities & adaptive load (detailed law)

- **Current state (2026-07-12 ‚Äî one shared `planWeek`, client == server, no exceptions):** the pure algorithm ‚Äî `angular/src/app/core/services/periodization-engine.ts` ‚Äî is mirrored server-side and both are wired together. Recovery and injury precedence are wired **in the engine** (client and server share the identical module), not in the `daily_protocols`/`ai-chat` generator (which remains for coach/AI features, not the athlete plan). The **week-planning orchestration itself** (schedule-aware intent hints ‚Üí `prescribeFor`√ó7 ‚Üí the ‚â•2 rest-day minimum ‚Üí PM second sessions) now lives in the engine as the pure `planWeek()`, so the client's `today` (= `weekAhead()[0]`), This Week, and the server all run the identical function ‚Äî the Today-vs-This-Week drift (a single-day `today` that skipped the week-level rest-minimum) is now structurally impossible. `planWeek` source‚áîport parity + the rest-minimum-on-day-0 invariant are locked in `tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`. **Backend-authoritative migration (2026-07-08, completed 2026-07-12):**
  - The engine is mirrored server-side (`netlify/functions/utils/periodization-engine.js`, generated via `npm run build:periodization-engine`) and proven **byte-for-byte identical** to the client on a 29-case golden-fixture matrix (`tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`).
  - A server endpoint (`/api/periodization-prescription`) assembles the same `PeriodizationInputs` the client wrapper builds ‚Äî schedule/phase (`schedule.js`'s extracted `getScheduleSnapshot()`), ACWR + readiness (`readiness_scores`), injuries (`getActiveInjuries` + a restriction-resolver mirroring `InjuryService.restrictions()`), position/age/bodyweight/season-calendar/team-practice (`users`/`athlete_training_config`), recent sessions (CNS spacing), **weather** (reuses `weather.js`'s `resolveTeamHomeCity`/`getWeatherData`) and **travel/acclimatization** (`athlete_travel_log`, mirroring `EventTravelService` exactly) ‚Äî then calls the ported engine. 31 unit tests lock in the assembly logic. Only `coachOverride`/`weeklyIntentHint`/`weeklyProgressionUnsafe` stay unwired (each the engine's own documented "no guard" default).
  - **`today` is the single source, always local:** `PeriodizationService.today` = `localPrescription()` = `weekAhead()[0]` ‚Äî one computation, instant and offline-safe (Law #4). The prior "prefer the server single-day prescription" model was retired: that endpoint could not apply the week-level passes (rest minimum / PM sessions), so it drifted from This Week on enforced-rest days. The server fetch is kept **only as a runtime drift canary** (`fetchServerPrescription` ‚Üí logs `periodization_server_match`/`periodization_server_mismatch` via `RemoteTelemetryService`); it never changes what `today` renders. ACWR/readiness are already server-canonical inputs, so nothing canonical is lost by rendering the local `planWeek` day 0.
  - **`weekAhead()` is NO LONGER an exception:** the server's `/api/periodization-prescription` now assembles a full 7-day input window and calls the same `planWeek()`, returning day 0 ‚Äî so the server is week-aware and identical to the client by construction. `weekAhead()` stays client-computed for the instant week-at-a-glance view (still no 7√ó latency), but it is the same algorithm the server runs.
  - **Population-aware ACWR classification ‚Äî client cohorts WIRED 2026-07-14 (audit batch 3; supersedes the 2026-07-08 "MOOT/orphaned" finding).** `EvidenceConfigService` now DERIVES the active preset instead of defaulting it: active `return_to_play_protocols` (status=active) ‚Üí `return_to_play_v1`; age < 18 ‚Üí `youth_flag_v1`; age ‚â• 35 ‚Üí `masters_flag_v1` (NEW ‚Äî ACWR 0.8/1.2/1.4, heuristic-conservative on top of the engine's age-scaled CNS windows); else adult. The pure rule is `derivePresetId(ageYears, hasActiveRtp)` (evidence-presets, unit-tested); age comes from the shared `ageYearsFromUserMetadata` util (same source as the engine's CNS scaling). `setActivePreset()` remains as an explicit override that wins over the derivation (`clearPresetOverride()` restores it). **Backend readiness is cohort-aware since batch 4 (same day):** `calc-readiness` resolves the cohort server-side (`utils/cohort.js`, drift-guarded byte-equal to the client) and scores workload + the safety trigger against the cohort bands; display lanes without athlete context stay adult + client tightening (LOGIC ¬ß10).
  - Do not remove the client engine (`localPrescription`, the fallback + `weekAhead`'s source) ‚Äî it stays load-bearing indefinitely, by design.
- Equipment inventory is stored on **`athlete_training_config.available_equipment`** (jsonb array of equipment ids) ‚Äî reuse this column; do **not** use the dead/missing `athlete_recovery_profiles`/`equipment_items` tables. Catalogue of ids+labels lives as reference data, never hardcoded into logic.
- **Equipment gate (LAW):** the engine may only recommend a modality the player actually owns/has access to (no Normatec without compression boots, ever).
- **Modality triggers (data-driven):** compression boots ‚Üí post high-load / ACWR spike / congested fixtures; massage gun ‚Üí pre-session activation or post-session localized tightness; stretching/mobility ‚Üí tightness, low-readiness, maintenance (always available, bodyweight); massage knives (IASTM) ‚Üí persistent localized tightness; foam roller ‚Üí general post-session soreness; physio referral ‚Üí severity threshold crossed **or** recurrence.
- **Self-report ‚Üí recalculation (Merlin loop, LAW):** a region tightness/soreness report (Wellness region selector or Merlin chat) is persisted to `daily_wellness_checkin.soreness_areas` **and** an active restriction in `athlete_injuries` (`recovery_status='active'`, `activity_restrictions[]`, short auto-expiry). The system **MUST recalculate** load/RPE/prescription ‚Äî never silently keep the old plan. **Injury/physio precedence overrides training (LAW):** a relevant signal down-regulates/removes sprint/high-intensity work for the affected region regardless of the periodization plan (competition/taper safety branches still apply). Every override is deterministic and logged (region, severity, source; intent before‚Üíafter).

### 5b. Daily-input forms ‚Äî prefill & non-destructive submit (detailed law)

Canonical lifecycle for every recurring input form (wellness check-in, supplements, post-event participation, self-report, RPE/session log). A form that opens with hardcoded defaults **and** submits via upsert silently destroys saved data on a no-touch resubmit ‚Äî **P0**.

1. **On open ‚Äî fetch** the existing record for the period from the canonical table (verified name ‚Äî `daily_wellness_checkin`, not `wellness_checkin`).
2. **Prefill** from SAVED values if a record exists; else a genuine empty/unset state ‚Äî never fabricated mid-range defaults that look real.
3. **Distinguish unset from a real value** (touched/dirty flag or null state); gate submit on it where a default could be mistaken for a real entry.
4. **On submit ‚Äî non-destructive:** never overwrite a saved column with an untouched default. Partial update of changed fields, or full prefill so the upsert carries real values. (`upsert_wellness_checkin` COALESCEs null‚Üíexisting, so sending only touched fields is safe there.)
5. **Failure:** if the prefill fetch fails, surface it and **block the destructive submit** ‚Äî do not fall back to defaults and write.

Reference implementations: the wellness check-in prefill `effect()` and `profile-edit.component.ts` prefill-from-GET. Append-only logs (hydration, training-session) must be **idempotent per natural key** (one `training_sessions` row per `user_id`+`session_date`+`session_type`) so a double submit updates rather than double-counts into ACWR.

- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî SHIPPED: "log your practice" nudge (honest fix for the "practices don't feed ACWR" gap).** Audit of the old A2 finding ("team practices contribute 0 to ACWR") showed it **overstated the gap**: `utils/acwr.js#computeSessionLoad` counts **any** logged session and practices ARE logged as `session_type='flag_football'` (live rows), so a logged practice **already feeds ACWR**; and `periodization-engine.ts addSecondSessions` already **skips practice days** (`if (teamPracticeFlags[i]) return p`). The only real gap is an **unlogged** practice ‚Äî and injecting a *fabricated estimated* practice load into ACWR is exactly what ¬ß4 forbids ("load is never fabricated from defaults"). So the honest fix is a **nudge**, not a calc change: new pure `angular/src/app/today/unlogged-practice.ts#resolveUnloggedPractice(trainingDays, loggedAt, now)` detects a today/yesterday practice day with no logged session (client has both ‚Äî `schedule.snapshot().trainingDays` + `periodization.recentSessions()` = last 4d completed sessions); Today shows a clock-iconed banner "Log yesterday's practice so your training load (ACWR) stays accurate" linking to `/training`. Suppressed once a session is logged for that day. `unlogged-practice.spec.ts` (7). Angular 504 / build (strict templates) / typecheck / lint green.

- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 5b ‚Äî cooler-hour time-shift ("train at 20:00 when it drops to ~27¬∞").** Completes Phase 5. **Audit:** the 5a heat guard already told the athlete to "train in the cooler hour" but `weather.js:115` fetched `current=` **only**, so the app had no hourly data to name that hour. **Fix:** `weather.js` now also pulls Open-Meteo **hourly** (`&hourly=‚Ä¶&forecast_days=2`, venue-local via `timezone=auto`), flattened by `parseHourly` into `{time,tempC,humidityPct,‚Ä¶}` and surfaced on `/api/weather` as `hourly[]`. New engine model: `WeatherInput.hourly`, `HourlyWeatherPoint`, `WeatherTimeShift`, `DailyPrescription.timeShift`, `PeriodizationInputs.preferredTrainingHour`. New pure `findCoolerHour(hourly, fromHour, currentWbgt)` ‚Äî evaluates WBGT at the athlete's training hour; if that hour is hot (‚â• WBGT_REDUCE 30¬∞) and a **materially cooler (‚â•1.5¬∞), comfortable (< 30¬∞)** hour exists later the **same day** (within a 6h wait, before a 21:00 evening cap), returns the **earliest** such hour with an athlete-facing message. `prescribeFor` attaches `timeShift` for an outdoor field session (advisory only ‚Äî the prescribed load is unchanged; skipped when injury forced a change or the day is rest). **No fabricated defaults:** no hourly / training-hour already fine / no cooler hour ‚Üí no suggestion. **Anchor:** server reads `team_training_days.time` (`parseTrainingHour` "HH:MM"‚Üíhour, venue-local, correct vs the server's UTC clock); client uses the current wall-clock hour (day-0 `date` is midnight). UI: Today's session card shows a clock-iconed banner with `timeShift.message`. **Parity:** no hourly in any fixture ‚Üí `timeShift` never set ‚Üí drift-parity + golden **byte-identical**; new `weather-timeshift.test.js` (7) locks `findCoolerHour` (suggests earliest comfortable hour / already-fine ‚Üí null / hot-all-evening ‚Üí null / past-cap ‚Üí null / no-data ‚Üí null) + a `prescribeFor` integration. Port regenerated; backend **989** / Angular **497** / build (strict templates) / typechecks / lint green. **Phase 5 (weather) COMPLETE.**
- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 5a ‚Äî weather guard rebuilt on WBGT √ó duration √ó intensity.** The gated audit's Phase 5 (weather), part a. **Audit:** the guard keyed on INTENT not heat strain (`OUTDOOR_INTENSE = {sprint,mixed,taper-prime}`, `periodization-engine.ts` guard) ‚Äî so a **75-min outdoor skills session at 33¬∞C was completely unguarded** while short sprints were scaled (the D13 inversion); thresholds were raw apparent-temp (28/32/35/38¬∞C) not WBGT; the scale action was a **flat 20% cut** regardless of session length; and humidity (fetched by `weather.js:115` as `relative_humidity_2m`) was **dropped** before the guard. **Fix:** new `approxWBGT(tempC, humidityPct)` (Australian BoM shade formula `0.567¬∑Ta + 0.393¬∑e + 3.94`, cited) ‚Üí the guard now uses **WBGT** with NATA/ACSM continuous-activity thresholds (27.8 scale / 30.0 relocate / 32.2 stop ¬∞C WBGT) when humidity is present, guards **all field intents incl. `technical`** (fixes the inversion), and **strain-scales the volume cut** by WBGT-band √ó duration √ó intensity (a long sprint in heat loses up to 50%, a short skills session ~20%). Humidity threaded end-to-end: `WeatherInput.humidityPct` (new) ‚Üê client `periodization.service` (`/api/weather` `d.humidity`) + server `resolveWeather` (`data.humidity`). **No fabricated defaults:** humidity absent ‚Üí byte-identical **legacy apparent-temp fallback**, and the athlete sees "29¬∞C WBGT" vs "33¬∞C feels-like" honestly. **Parity:** the drift-parity weather fixtures (humidity-less) stay **byte-identical**; new `tests/unit/weather-wbgt-guard.test.js` (7) locks the formula, the humidity-flips-risk case (30¬∞C @ 40% benign vs @ 70% STOP), the technical-now-guarded inversion, and the duration√óintensity scaling. Port regenerated; backend 982 / Angular 497 / typechecks / lint green. **Phase 5b (next, pending):** Open-Meteo **hourly** forecast + the **time-shift** recommendation ("train at 20:00 when it drops to ~27¬∞C") ‚Äî the reason text already says "train in the cooler hour" but the app has no hourly data yet to name it.
- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî HOTFIX: weather-provider failure 500'd `/api/periodization-prescription`.** Live production 500 on Today/Training (blanked the athlete's plan). Root cause: `getWeatherData` returns `null` on ANY provider/geocoding failure (`weather.js:166` ‚Äî Open-Meteo / Nominatim timeout/error), but `resolveWeather` then dereferenced `data.temp` on that null ‚Üí `TypeError` ‚Üí the assembly `Promise.all` rejected ‚Üí the whole prescription 500'd. Weather is a **non-critical guard** input; a provider hiccup must degrade to "no weather", never take down the plan. **Fix:** guard the null (`!data || data.temp == null`) + wrap `resolveWeather` in a `try/catch` that logs (`periodization_weather_resolve_failed`) and returns `null`, so neither a null result nor an unexpected throw (home-city geocoding, provider) can 500 the endpoint. `tests/unit/periodization-weather-resilience.test.js` reproduces the crash and locks null/throw/geocode-throw ‚Üí resolves-null, real weather ‚Üí mapped. Shipped as a standalone hotfix (PR #204, off `main`) so it reached production immediately, decoupled from the gated Phase 4 taper PR (#203, which also carries it). Backend 975 / functions-typecheck / lint green.
- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî SHIPPED: Periodization Phase 4b ‚Äî two-layer, level-graduated taper (`taper_rules` wired).** Follow-on to 4a below, resolving the ¬ß4 issue that `taper_rules` (curated, Bosquet/Mujika-cited) was **unwired**. **Two-layer model** (user-directed): the engine is deterministic and runs the taper on a **materialized `TaperRuleset`** ‚Äî never raw DB rows. Layer 1 (engine): `EMBEDDED_TAPER_RULES` (version `v1-2026-07-13`, mirrors the live table) is the pure/offline default; `taperLevelFor` maps the 7-value `CompetitionLevel`‚Üí the curated 5 (club‚Üílocal, continental‚Üíinternational, olympic‚Üíworld, unknown‚Üínational); `resolveTaperTargets(ruleset, level, isFinalThird)` holds intensity at `baseline√óintensityRetention` (RPE‚â•7, never mobility) and cuts VOLUME by the level's `volumeFloorPct` with a deeper final-third (√ó0.66). Layer 2 (server): `periodization-prescription.js#resolveTaperRuleset` reads active `taper_rules`, materializes them into the **same** schema (source `live`), passes via `inputs.taperRuleset`; **falls back to embedded** on any error / partial-vocabulary / malformed number (never a bad taper). Response carries `taperPolicy {version, source}` for provenance. So the taper is now **graduated by event level** ‚Äî a local game (floor 0.70/ret 0.90 ‚Üí RPE7, 42min) tapers far less than a World peak (floor 0.50/ret 1.00 ‚Üí RPE8, 30min), and the national default = 33min/6reps front, 22min/4reps final (this supersedes 4a's flat international-tier numbers below). Migration `taper_rules_version_column` added `version` (default `v1-2026-07-13`) so live/embedded stay in lock-step; live introspection confirms **live == embedded == the golden test** (0 behavior change from wiring, pure additive provenance + future coach-tunability seam ‚Äî the resolver already accepts an override ruleset). Tests: `taper-two-layer.test.js` (level map + embedded golden + resolver graduation/retention/min-reps-floor/override) + `resolveTaperRuleset` hydration cases (full‚Üílive, partial‚Üínull, malformed‚Üínull, error‚Üínull) in `periodization-prescription.test.js`; drift-parity gained a level-graduation case. Port regenerated; snapshot+types+docs:regen refreshed (176 tables, 0 DRIFT). Backend 971 / Angular 497 / functions-typecheck / lint all green.
- **2026-07-13 ‚Äî FIXED: Periodization Phase 4a ‚Äî the taper now holds INTENSITY and cuts VOLUME (rubric B6).** The gated periodization audit's Phase 4 (graduated gameday ramp + taper). **Audit finding:** the two ramp axes are structurally sound ‚Äî the season macro-phase (Phase 3 `phaseSessionModel`: offseason=GPP ‚Üí preseason=build ‚Üí inseason=maintain ‚Üí peak=game-speed) and the game-proximity micro-phase (`schedule-resolver.ts` `resolvePhase`: transition >14d ‚Üí accumulation 2‚Äì14d ‚Üí taper ‚Üí taper-prime ‚â§24h ‚Üí competition, importance-tiered so a regular league game gets a 2-day mini-taper while world/olympic events get the 7‚Äì14-day full taper). **But the taper itself VIOLATED rubric item 6** ("volume ‚àí40‚Äì60%, intensity MAINTAINED or raised ‚Äî if any taper path reduces intensity, flag as VIOLATES"): against the accumulation `sprint` baseline (RPE 8 / 60 min / 10 reps, `periodization-engine.ts:1917-1923`), every taper path **cut intensity** ‚Äî `TAPER_CONFIG.individual.regular` was RPE 6, `.final` was **`mobility` RPE 4** (deleting all CNS/velocity work in the final 48h ‚Äî the textbook detraining error, Mujika & Padilla 2003), and `PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS.taper`/`taper_final` were RPE 6/5 (base 7). **Fix (intensity held at baseline, only volume cut):** `individual.regular` ‚Üí sprint, **RPE 8**, 30 min, 5 reps (‚àí50% vol); `individual.final` ‚Üí **sprint** (not mobility), **RPE 8**, 20 min, 3 reps (progressive, deeper volume cut, still near-max efforts); practice `taper`/`taper_final` ‚Üí **RPE 7** held, minutes 90‚Üí60/45. `taper-prime` (‚â§24h game-eve primer) left as-is ‚Äî it already keeps velocity strides (4 reps) and is the opener, not a taper block. **Parity harness:** `tests/unit/periodization-drift-parity.test.js` gained a Phase-4 block with B6-invariant assertions (velocity preserved / RPE ‚â• baseline / volume ‚àí40-60% / progressive) that FAILED before and PASS after; the two non-taper golden snapshots stayed **byte-identical** (git diff = 64 insertions, 0 deletions) proving no collateral drift; the `phase-taper` golden fixture in `periodization-parity.test.js` updated to the new values. - **2026-07-13 ‚Äî FIXED: warm-up is dynamic RAMP; static stretching retired as a default (tightness triage added).** The recovery-day warm-up template (`daily-protocol-training-logic.js buildWarmupTemplate`, `recovery` variant) embedded four STATIC holds as defaults (Hip 90/90, Pigeon Pose, Seated Hamstring, Hip-Flexor Lunge ‚âà 450 s). Static stretching raises stretch TOLERANCE, not muscle length (Weppler 2010), gives no recovery/DOMS benefit (Afonso 2021), and long pre-activity holds transiently cut strength/power ‚Äî so those four were replaced with DYNAMIC through-range mobility (90/90 Hip Switches, Cossack Squats, Straight-Leg Kicks, Walking Hip-Flexor Lunges), preserving the exact 1500 s (25 min) budget. The `fitness`/`field` variants were already dynamic RAMP. Static stretching now enters a session ONLY via the new `tightnessTriageStretch(region, {soreRegions, injuryRegions})` ‚Äî a targeted <30 s hold for a genuine documented ROM restriction, hard-guarded to return null on acutely sore / injured tissue (McHugh & Tyler 2019). `tests/unit/warmup-tightness-triage.test.js` (9) locks the dynamic conversion + budget + the contraindication guard. **Wiring hook (honest):** there is no live "tightness"/ROM input yet (`daily_wellness_checkin` has `soreness_areas` = a *contraindication* here, not an indication), so the triage helper is exported + tested + documented, ready to wire when a ROM-restriction signal exists. Backend 905 green; typecheck + lint clean.

New `tests/unit/recovery-recommendations-evidence.test.js` (6) locks the corrected behaviour; `recovery-triggers`/`recovery-validation`/`recovery-protocols` still green; typecheck + lint clean.
