- **2026-07-12 ‚Äî Single day-truth: shared `planWeek()` (Today / This Week / server can't drift).** Live bug: the Today hero showed a *Mixed session* on a day This Week marked *Rest*. Root cause: the week-level passes (the ‚â•2 rest-day minimum + PM second-sessions) were applied only by the client `weekAhead()`, while `today` preferred a single-day server prescription (`/api/periodization-prescription`) ‚Äî and a single day cannot make a week-level rest decision. **Fix:** extracted the week-planning orchestration into a pure `planWeek()` in the parity-tested engine (`periodization-engine.ts` ‚Üí generated port); the client `today` = `weekAhead()[0]`, and the server endpoint now assembles a 7-day input window and runs the identical `planWeek`, returning day 0 (per-day phase via the newly-exported `schedule.js#resolvePhase`). So `today`, This Week, the COMPOSE intent handed to `daily-protocol`, and the server all read ONE computation ‚Äî drift is structurally impossible. The old server-preference + verified-match gate is retired; the server fetch is kept only as a runtime drift canary. `planWeek` source‚áîport parity + a rest-minimum-on-day-0 invariant added to `tests/unit/periodization-port-parity.test.js`. Fixed alongside: the daily-protocol duplicated every gym split-block exercise into a consolidated "Main Session" (removed ‚Äî the split blocks ARE the session), and the hero band shows "Rest day". Backend 883 / Angular 182 periodization tests green. **Next (Track 2, planned):** `mapIntentToSession` still collapses rest/recovery/mobility/travel/competition into one `recovery` focus ‚Äî making each day type distinct + surfacing tunable recovery-modality protocols (compression mmHg/min/√óweek, percussion) is the follow-on.
- **2026-07-12 ‚Äî Tissue Load Engine, Phase 2 (exercise library + tissue/rate schema), IN PROGRESS.** Grounded on live introspection: the `exercises` table had **286 rows but ZERO `category='rehab'`** ‚Äî so the RTP rehab block was empty in production (an injured athlete's rehab session had no exercises to draw from), and there was no seated-vs-standing calf distinction and no loading-rate field anywhere (the two schema gaps the Phase-0 audit called load-bearing). **Schema (live + migration `20260712120000_tissue_load_exercise_attributes.sql`):** additive nullable columns on `exercises` ‚Äî `tissue_targets text[]` (canonical tissue-node ids; the safety filter's real substrate, replacing name-keyword matching), `contraction_type`, `joint_emphasis` (knee_bent=soleus / knee_straight=gastroc), `loading_rate_band` (the rate axis, distinct from volume), `peak_load_bw`, `evidence_tier`, `rehab_stage` ‚Äî with CHECK-constrained vocabularies + a GIN index on `tissue_targets`. **Authoring pipeline:** `database/library/tissue-registry.mjs` (the anatomical graph ‚Äî tissue nodes typed tendon/muscle/ligament/bone with cross-region couples), `database/library/families.mjs` (curated movement families + a variant expander), `scripts/build-exercise-library.mjs` (expands ‚Üí validates enums+tissue ids ‚Üí emits the idempotent `database/seed-exercise-library.generated.sql` + manifest). **First tranche live:** 48 evidence-based rehab/isometric exercises the DB completely lacked ‚Äî the calf‚ÄìAchilles complex (seated/soleus + standing/gastroc raises, eccentric heel drops, isometric holds), hamstring (Nordic/Askling L-protocol/iso), patellar-tendon rate ladder (isometric‚ÜíHSR‚Üídecline), Copenhagen adduction levels 1‚Äì3 + the squeeze test, ankle balance progressions, tibialis/MTSS endurance. Live count 286‚Üí**333**; `category='rehab'` 0‚Üí**38**; 48 tissue-tagged (17 correctly flagged Achilles loaders). On-conflict enriches tissue attrs only (existing curated rows like the Strength-category Nordic keep their curation, gain tissue metadata). **Still open (this phase):** backfill `tissue_targets` on the remaining ~285 existing rows; rewire `isExerciseSafeForInjuries` + the RTP rehab query to key on `tissue_targets`/`rehab_stage` (currently name-keyword + `category='rehab'`); a CI test asserting no plantarflexor loader passes the achilles filter via tissue_targets; regenerate `supabase-types.ts` + `live-schema.snapshot.json` + `docs:regen` for the new columns; continue authoring families toward the 1000+ target (general strength/power/plyo-rate-ladder/speed/agility/mobility/conditioning/position/arm-care). The Phase-1 keyword filter stays as the safety stopgap until the tissue-graph rewire lands.

- **2026-07-12 ‚Äî Tissue Load Engine, Phase 1 (stop lying to players) + supplement behaviour insights.** Closes the self-report ‚Üí recalculation loop's trust bugs (audit in `docs/ground-truth/`, forthcoming). **(P1) Today "Body check" now persists for real:** the region chips (`today.component.ts`) previously mutated local signals only while the UI claimed "Logged ‚Ä¶ coach notified" ‚Äî a Law 7 fabrication that let a player train through a reported Achilles/hamstring niggle believing it had been logged. They now call the SAME `InjuryService.report()` ‚Üí `POST /api/athlete-injuries` path as Wellness (one code path), with a "Log it" button and a state machine (`idle/saving/saved/error`) so the "Logged" copy only ever renders after the `athlete_injuries` write lands; failure shows an error, never a success claim. Region labels map to the server's canonical region sets (added `finger` to `UPPER_BODY`). **(P1) RTP protocol now renders:** `generateReturnToPlayProtocol` returned a flat `{‚Ä¶protocol, exercises}` shape the client's block-keyed model (`protocol.models.ts`) couldn't read ‚Üí injured athletes saw "No specific exercises for today's plan" while the rehab session sat unrendered in the DB. It now returns via `getProtocol()` ‚Üí the same block-keyed shape; added `rehab_progression`/`evening_mobility` block keys end-to-end (they were already in the DB CHECK since 20260328, just dropped by the response transformer); response carries `is_return_to_play`. Client `ProtocolService` surfaces `pendingMessage` for the 202 coach-review/injury-gate responses (Training shows the review notice instead of the generic empty state). **(P2) Region safety filter fixed:** `REGION_KEYWORDS` (`daily-protocol-blocks.js`) treated `achilles` as `['achilles']`, so a "Standing Calf Stretch" (the only calf row in the library seed, and knee-straight/gastroc-biased) PASSED the achilles filter ‚Äî the most dangerous line in the codebase. The calf‚ÄìAchilles complex is now one unit both directions (+ knee‚Üîpatellar, foot‚Üîplantar/heel, shin‚Üîtibialis, hamstring‚Üînordic), via a new exported `keywordsForRegion()`; RTP rehab selection prefers region-matching exercises before generic filler. **(P3) One pain-trigger threshold:** server `> 3` vs client `>= 6` meant a 4‚Äì5 soreness never reached the safety-override path ‚Äî now one canonical `PAIN_TRIGGER_THRESHOLD` (`safety-override.js`) mirrored in `wellness.constants.ts` (`SORENESS_PAIN_TRIGGER`), drift-guarded by `tests/unit/soreness-threshold-parity.test.js`. Plus: high soreness (`>= WELLNESS.HIGH_PAIN_THRESHOLD`) with no region flagged and no active injury now PROMPTS a body check on Wellness (honest copy: the slider alone doesn't change the session) ‚Äî one soft nudge, "Log check-in anyway" proceeds. **Concurrency:** the `createProtocolGenerationRequest` guard moved ahead of the injury branch so RTP and normal generation share one guard in the same order (RTP's own `daily_protocols` upsert + `protocol_exercises` delete/insert used to race outside it). **Supplement behaviour (new):** `GET /api/supplements/insights` detects an established supplement habit that lapsed (‚â•5 taken days in the prior 30 + none in the last 7) ‚Üí deduped in-app `notification_type='supplement_lapse'` restock nudge (partner offer is an owner-set product value in `supplements.js`); the Supplements screen adds usage-/phase-aware creatine + beta-alanine coaching (evidence-honest: loading optional, no mandatory cycling, chronic dosing) driven by real log history + `schedule.currentPhase()`. Backend 842 / Angular 497 tests green (added `supplement-insights`, `region-safety-keywords`, `rtp-response-blocks`, `soreness-threshold-parity`, `ui-truthfulness`); tsc/tooling-lint clean; the 4 Angular template lint warnings pre-date this and are untouched. **Deliberately deferred to later phases (approved plan):** the typed anatomical tissue graph (replaces `REGION_KEYWORDS` wholesale), the two-axis guard (`sprintVolumePct` vs `plyoRatePct` ‚Äî no loading-rate substrate exists in the schema yet), symptom-behaviour capture (pain timing / 24h response / week-trend), and a 1000+ evidence-mapped exercise library. Phase-1 keyword expansion is a correct stopgap, not the target model.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Data-viz modernization (Pass B of the front-end polish protocol): one signature gradient across every line chart.** Visual rendering only ‚Äî data, scales, thresholds, tooltips, aria and every coordinate are unchanged. New tokens `--chart-stroke-a/b` (the existing brand pair), `--chart-area-a/b`, `--chart-glow`. Applied to all four chart surfaces: **readiness-trend** (canonical ‚Äî gradient stroke, two-stop area fade, glowing endpoint that KEEPS its zone color, tabular-num axis ticks), **acwr-trend** (gradient stroke + glow endpoint; sweet band 0.8‚Äì1.3 + danger 1.5 threshold untouched), **Today micro-sparkline** (gradient stroke only ‚Äî no glow at 26px), **monitoring-report wellness sparkline** (leveled to the same treatment). AA graphics contrast verified: stroke stops 10.58:1 (#00E07A) and 5.69:1 (#8B7CFF) on `--surface` (needs ‚â•3:1). Zone meaning stays in state colors + numeric labels ‚Äî the gradient never encodes state. No draw-in animation (stroke-dashoffset is a paint property ‚Äî barred by the Pass A motion rules); heatmap/coverage grids deliberately excluded (their cell colors encode data). See `REDESIGN-NOTES.md`.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Interaction layer (Pass A of the front-end polish protocol): motion + state tokens applied at the design-system layer.** UI only, zero functionality change. New tokens `--t-entrance`/`--t-stagger`/`--ease-out` join the existing `--t-fast/--t-med/--ease`. `_system.scss` gains: a **baseline element-level interaction layer** (`button/a/[role=button]/select/summary` get one shared paint+transform transition, generic press scale, focus-visible ring fallback, disabled cursor ‚Äî element-level so it ripples into the scoped `.td`/`.tr` redesigns without per-screen tuning); hover states for `.btn` secondary/ghost/danger (gated off disabled), `.chip`, `.icon-btn`, inactive `.tabbar` links; `.linkcard` hover lift (tokens replace a hardcoded `0.15s`); `.fab` hover/press; and **staggered entrance motion** for `.screen > *` (28 templates) + `.stack > *` (3) ‚Äî 360ms fade-rise, 45ms stagger capped at 6, `backwards` fill so no persistent transform (protects `position:fixed` descendants), fires once per mount. The global reduced-motion guard additionally zeroes `animation-delay` so staggered content is instantly visible. Verified: stylelint/prettier/build/497 tests green; Playwright pass at 380/768/1440; reduced-motion emulation shows content at first paint (`getAnimations()` empty, opacity 1); focus ring confirmed (`--focus` 3px accent). Playwright VRT disables animations by default ‚Üí snapshots unaffected. Deliberate non-motion logged in `REDESIGN-NOTES.md`.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî UI spacing audit: fixed label/value text jammed with no space (Angular whitespace strip).** Reported symptom: "Hydration9.3 L today" rendered with no gap. Root cause: Angular's default `preserveWhitespaces: false` **removes whitespace-only text nodes between two inline elements** ‚Äî so `<b>Hydration</b> <small>‚Ä¶` (and even `&ensp;`, a collapsible Unicode space) lose the gap. Fixes: `&nbsp;` (a preserved space) between `<b>Hydration</b>` and its value in **wellness** + **gameday**; `&nbsp;` in gameday's "Game schedule ‚Ä¶ games entered"; flex+`gap` on roster's `.inj-meta` (athlete name + grade/status pills were abutting); `margin-left` on Today's quote `.who` (author "‚Äî ‚Ä¶" hugged the quote). **Not bugs (left as-is):** value+unit stat chips (`3.2L`, `315g`, `8h` ‚Äî intentional), `.tiles` stat blocks (`.tiles b` is `display:block` ‚Üí value stacks over label), `.row` pairs (`justify-content:space-between` spreads them), and text like "0 L logged" / "0% done" (the space lives inside a text node with content, which Angular keeps). **Rule for future templates:** a visible space between two inline elements must be `&nbsp;`/CSS margin/`gap`, never a bare inter-tag space.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Onboarding team+role selection + honest "partial" check-in state; diagnosed why live ‚âÝ the redesign.** Root cause of "the live Today doesn't match the mockup": the signed-in account (`aljkous@gmail.com`) had **no team membership** ‚Äî an invite-less signup fell through onboarding without ever joining a team (role/team came ONLY from `accept-invitation`). Teamless ‚Üí `/api/weather` resolves no `home_city` ‚Üí no weather chip; `schedule.upcoming` finds no events ‚Üí no Season Plan; not on the roster. (Also: every `competition_event` in the DB is in the past, so the Season Plan timeline needs future events regardless ‚Äî the mockup's Elite8/Gameday entries are demo data.) **Fixes:** (1) New `netlify/functions/team-join.js` (`GET` lists joinable teams, `POST` joins with a chosen role). **Security model:** self-declared staff roles join `role_approval_status='pending_approval'` and gain **no** health-data access (stays behind `can_staff_read_athlete` + consent); players join `approved` (own data only); owner/admin can't be self-granted; writes self-scoped to the authed `userId` via service role. Staff joins also set `users.onboarding_completed` (players get it from `player-settings` at the end of their flow). (2) `onboarding.component` reworked into a **role-aware wizard**: a `team + role` first step (loads teams), then players run the full athlete flow (position/physicals/season/training ‚Üí join ‚Üí player-settings ‚Üí `/today`) while staff get a short confirm ‚Üí join ‚Üí `/staff`. (3) **Partial check-in coverage** on Today ‚Äî the grid now shows an honest `partial` state: a `daily_wellness_checkin` row missing a core driver (sleep/energy/stress/soreness) is amber `partial`, a full row (or a scored day) is `done`, no row is `miss`; derived from the already-loaded `WellnessService.wellnessData()` ‚Äî nothing fabricated (the card's promise holds). `%coverage` now counts any check-in (done+partial); missed + partial broken out; streak still counts full days only. **Live data set up** (per user): renamed the club team ‚Üí **International Frogs** and added `aljkous@gmail.com` as an active **player/Center** so weather + roster populate. Backend lint + base-handler guard green; onboarding spec rewritten (5 tests, incl. staff‚Üí`/staff`, players‚Üí`/today`, P0 no-navigate-on-write-failure); 497 Angular tests green; build/lint/prettier clean.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Today redesign follow-up: wired the components I'd first over-simplified.** On review, three mockup pieces had been flattened away; each is now wired to a real signal, not fabricated. (1) **Phased season timeline** ‚Äî the `timeline` computed now returns `{ segments, dots, months }`. Segments are colour-coded phase bars (**build ‚Üí taper ‚Üí in-season**) derived from the real upcoming events + the engine's own taper rule (locks ~6 days before the first event); a `.tl-legend` under the track names each phase with its date range and a **"now"** pill on the active segment. No fabricated phase dates ‚Äî every boundary is a real event date or today. (2) **Fuel nutri-strip** ‚Äî a new `nutriStrip` computed emits contextual chips each mapped to a server-canonical prescription signal: **Carb-load** (driver event inside 48h, from `hoursUntilNextEvent`/`driverEvent`), **Recovery plate** (from `recoveryEmphasis` high/critical), **Free-meal window** (rest/recovery intent with no game imminent). (3) **Electrolyte / sweat note** ‚Äî a new `electroNote` computed surfaces the real heat modifier (`weatherAdjustment.heatLoadFactor > 1` ‚Üí sodium guidance with the actual factor) or gameday sweat guidance (`intent === "competition"`). All three are display-only derivations of kept-engine outputs; the canonical numbers are unchanged. New scoped styles (`.phase*`, `.tl-legend`, `.lg`, `.nutri-strip`, `.nutri-chip*`, `.electro*`) under `.td`; used the already-registered `Droplet` icon. Build/lint/prettier clean, 496 Angular tests green, icon guard green.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Training page redesigned into a session runner (from the mockup).** Rebuilt `training.component` (`.ts`/`.html` + new scoped `.scss`) with an app-bar + section tabs (Today/Schedule/Programs/Library). **Today tab:** a session hero (intent band + date + weather chip + reasoning + RPE/min/sprints/fluid stats + a live session-progress bar), a **session-blocks accordion driven by the real `ProtocolService` blocks** ‚Äî each exercise is checkable (local runner state) with auto-advance to the next incomplete block, an expandable detail (`howText`/`feelText`), and a per-exercise video modal (`app-yt-video`); the QB arm-care card still self-gates below. A right rail (Conditions from the periodization weather, a Fuel cross-link to Today so hydration/supplements have one home, and the This-week plan from `weekAhead()`), and an **in-flow** completion bar (progress + RPE/duration steppers ‚Üí logs actual RPE √ó duration to the training log = the ACWR feed). Schedule/Programs/Library tabs render upcoming events / an explainer / the video grid. Server-canonical numbers rendered, never re-derived; ticking an exercise is local UI state. Styles component-scoped under `.tr`. **The completion bar is in-flow (not `position:fixed`)** so it never overlaps the app shell's mobile bottom-nav / FAB. Build/lint/prettier clean, 496 Angular tests green, icon guard green.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî Today page redesigned (from the provided mockup).** Rebuilt `today.component` (`.ts`/`.html` + new scoped `.scss`) into the richer layout: a photo hero band (greeting + daily-seeded rotating quote + live weather chip + notifications/avatar + team identity), season plan (real upcoming-event chips + an event-driven timeline computed from the real schedule, no fabricated phase dates), today's session card (real prescription: intent/why/RPE/min/sprints/fluid + a live-weather conditions line), a 28-day check-in coverage grid + ACWR-reliability bar (both from `readiness.history`), four tracking tiles (readiness + sparkline, sleep from `wellness.latestWellnessEntry`, ACWR, body-mass honest "sync" prompt), an interactive body-check (injury flags + severity), and fuel + hydration + evidence-graded supplement stack. SERVER-CANONICAL numbers (prescription/readiness/ACWR/weather/schedule) are rendered, never re-derived; the added presentational sections (quote, body-check, hydration tally, supplement stack) are local UI state to wire to their own endpoints in follow-ups. Styles are component-scoped (mockup tokens under `.td`, override globals via view encapsulation) so nothing leaks. Registered `Cloud`/`RefreshCw` icons; dropped the Today session-video (not in the redesign ‚Äî still in Training). Build/lint/prettier clean, 496 Angular tests green, icon-registration guard green.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî live production errors reported from the deployed site: two fixed, one bug-class flagged.** (1) **`/api/privacy-settings` ‚Üí 500 `database_error` on every save** (broke the new blood-consent toggle + all privacy toggles). Root cause: the endpoint used `getSupabaseClient()` ‚Äî the **anon** client (no token) ‚Äî but `privacy_settings` RLS grants only to `authenticated` (`user_id = auth.uid()`), so anon reads/writes were all denied. Fixed ‚Üí `supabaseAdmin` (matches `player-settings.js` + every other user-write endpoint; verified live that a service-role upsert succeeds). (2) **`/api/weekend-games` ‚Üí 502 on every call** (Monday wellness "did you play games?" flow). Root cause: `baseHandler` **throws `"functionName is required"` before its try/catch** when `functionName` is missing, and weekend-games passed only `{ handler }` ‚Üí unhandled throw ‚Üí 502. Fixed by adding `functionName`/`allowedMethods`/`rateLimitType`/`requireAuth`. Same latent 502 fixed in the orphaned `auth-reset-password.js` (also missing `functionName`; its `allowUnauthenticated` key was a no-op, so it also wrongly required auth ‚Äî set `requireAuth:false`). Added `tests/unit/base-handler-config.test.js` guarding the whole class. (3) **`account-deletion.js` had the same anon-client bug (FIXED).** Its deletion-request read/write used the anon `getSupabaseClient()` from `./supabase-client.js`; `account_deletion_requests` RLS has 0 anon policies (verified) so those ops failed. Switched to `supabaseAdmin` (self-scoped to `userId`; the actual deletion RPC already used admin). **Scoping the class precisely:** there are TWO same-named `getSupabaseClient` ‚Äî the one in `utils/supabase-client.js` (re-exported by `supabase-client.js`) returns the **anon** client, but the one in `utils/auth-helper.js` returns **`supabaseAdmin`**. Only privacy-settings + account-deletion imported the anon one; `accept-invitation` / `team-invite` / `player-programs` / `auth-me` / `validate-invitation` / `auth-login` import from `auth-helper.js` ‚Üí admin ‚Üí **not affected** (an earlier draft of this note wrongly flagged them). Net: the anon-client bug is fully resolved. (4) **YouTube `hqdefault.jpg` ‚Üí 504** is external (YouTube thumbnail CDN timing out on a video-heavy screen) ‚Äî not a code bug; a lazy-load + fallback would reduce the console noise.

- **2026-07-10 ‚Äî E2E flow audit (all roles): fixed staff monitoring links stranding staff in the athlete shell.** Traced every role's journey (login‚Üílanding‚Üíscreens‚Üíactions) + cross-checked all `routerLink`/`navigate` targets against the route table. **Found + fixed:** the two staff monitoring entry points I added this session pointed at the athlete-shell copies of their routes ‚Äî `athlete-detail` ‚Üí `/monitoring/:id` and staff `more` ‚Üí `/team-monitoring` (both children of the athlete `ShellComponent`). A coach/physio tapping either left the staff shell for the athlete shell (athlete bottom-nav, no way back to roster). Fixed by hosting both inside the staff shell (`/staff/monitoring/:id`, `/staff/team-monitoring`, staffGuard-gated) and retargeting the links; the athlete-shell copies stay for the legit in-athlete-view path (`/reports` ‚Üí `/team-monitoring`, `/monitoring` self). **Clean elsewhere:** all static + dynamic nav targets resolve (no dead links ‚Üí silent `**`‚Üítoday redirect); login redirects by team_members role; `goAthlete()`‚Üí`/today` is the intentional "My athlete view"; squad-monitoring reachable by all staff (`HEALTH_DATA_ACCESS_ROLES` covers coach/physio/nutrition/psych); monitoring service/component are null-guarded (catchError‚Üínull). No type errors (tsc app+spec+functions+strictTemplates all clean). **Also found + fixed (render sweep):** ran a per-screen render pass (dev auth off, so every athlete route loads) collecting page/console errors ‚Äî 21/22 clean, but **`/profile` threw** `The "log-out" icon has not been provided by any available icon providers` (lucide's strict provider throws AT RUNTIME ‚Äî invisible to build/tsc ‚Äî so the Sign-out button's `log-out` icon had shipped broken on every user's profile). Registered `LogOut` in `app.config.ts`; swept all static + dynamic icon usages ‚Äî `log-out` was the only miss. Added `icon-registration.spec.ts` (scans every template's static `name="‚Ä¶"` against the registered `pick({})` set) so this runtime-only class can't regress.

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî FIXED (was: `calc-readiness.js` computed daily session load inline instead of via canonical `computeSessionLoad`).** Surfaced while scoping the daily-load calendar. `utils/acwr.js` exports `computeSessionLoad(session)` ‚Äî the single definition (`workload` if real, else `rpe √ó duration_minutes`, else 0) ‚Äî and **8** functions use it (compute-acwr, training-metrics, training-plan, daily-training, coach-core, training-stats-enhanced, smart-training-recommendations, acwr.js). **`calc-readiness.js` is the only load/ACWR consumer that does NOT** (line ~483): it builds `loadsByDay` with inline `duration * rpe`, which (a) **ignores a stored `workload`** (canonical prefers it) and (b) adds an **`intensity_level` fallback** when `rpe` is null (canonical does not ‚Äî "load is never fabricated from defaults"). Since calc-readiness's ACWR feeds the **readiness score** (safety-relevant), a future session whose stored `workload ‚âÝ rpe√óduration` (e.g. an imported wearable/adjusted load) would make the readiness ACWR silently disagree with the ACWR screen + every other consumer. **Live impact today = 0** (verified: of 16 sessions, 15 have `workload>0` and **all** equal `rpe√óduration`; 0 `intensity_level`-only rows; 0 sessions in the last 28d so ACWR is null/insufficient anyway). **Fixed** (2026-07-09, user-directed): the inline block is now the exported pure helper `buildLoadsByDay(sessions)` calling canonical `computeSessionLoad`, so all load/ACWR consumers share one definition. Safety treatment done ‚Äî `tests/unit/calc-readiness-load-parity.test.js` reproduces the retired inline calc and **proves byte-identical output on today's live shape** (`workload == rpe√óduration`), while pinning the two intended deltas: a stored `workload ‚âÝ rpe√óduration` is now honoured (was ignored), and the `intensity_level`-as-RPE fallback is dropped (0 live rows; aligns with every other consumer ‚Äî if `intensity_level` should count as load, that belongs in `computeSessionLoad` for everyone, not one endpoint). Full backend suite green (incl. `readiness-formula-unification`).
- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî FIXED (was: per-athlete `monitoring/:id` staff report dark, app-layer role source diverged from the authoritative one).** Investigating "coach athlete-detail trend parity" surfaced this. The single-athlete role-shaped report (`monitoring-report.js` ‚Üí `MonitoringReportComponent`, route `monitoring/:id`) is (a) **navigationally orphaned** ‚Äî nothing links to it (only the squad table `/team-monitoring` is linked), and (b) **non-functional for staff**: its app-layer `resolveRequesterRole` reads `team_member_roles` (head_coach/sc_coach/physio), which is **empty in live (0 rows) with no sync trigger** from `team_members` ‚Äî so every staff caller resolves to `role: null` ‚Üí 403; only an athlete viewing their _own_ report (`role: "self"`) works. This is the **same bug-class as the login-routing bug** (role read from the wrong source): the RLS backstop `can_staff_read_athlete` correctly uses `team_members`, but the endpoint bypasses RLS via `supabaseAdmin` and gates purely on the empty `team_member_roles`. **Do not naively "fix" by pointing it at `team_members`:** (1) the endpoint's _only_ authorization is the role check ‚Äî there is **no app-layer athlete-consent gate** (only `buildWearableRaw` self-filters `consent_state='granted'`), so enabling staff would emit load/bloodwork/physio **without a consent check** (RLS, which would enforce it, is bypassed); (2) `team_members.role` has no head_coach-vs-sc_coach distinction, so the coach‚Üílens mapping (and whether a generic "coach" should see load at all) is a **product/legal decision** on health-data exposure. **Recommended path (deferred to Option 2):** build coach trend parity on the _proven_ consent path the working `/team-monitoring` squad table already uses ‚Äî `getUserRole` (team_members) + explicit `canCoachViewPerformance`/`canCoachViewWellness` per athlete ‚Äî via a new consent-gated per-athlete readiness+ACWR **history** endpoint (none exists today; existing history endpoints return only the logged-in user's own series), reusing the presentational `app-acwr-trend`. Until then the coach athlete-detail keeps its 2 current-value chips (honest, consent-safe). **RESOLVED 2026-07-09 (club-owner directive: "physio and coaches see full clinical data, all the data"):** `resolveRequesterRole` now reads the authoritative `team_members` (active same-team) via the pure, tested `lensForRoles` ‚Äî `physiotherapist`|`coach` ‚Üí the full clinical lens (`physio`; all data ‚Äî raw bloodwork/wearable/load/physio), `nutritionist`/`psychologist`/`player` ‚Üí null/403 (they use their own lanes). Verified live: coach + physio resolve to full clinical, the others to 403 (rolled-back sim). Reachable now via a "Full monitoring report" link added to the coach + physio lanes of staff `athlete-detail` (route `/monitoring/:id`, athlete-shell `authGuard`). Test: `tests/unit/monitoring-report-role.test.js` (5). **GDPR / consent:** raw **bloodwork** (special-category medical data) is now **athlete-consent-gated** even on the full clinical lens (2026-07-09, per owner request) ‚Äî a staff viewer sees the values only when the athlete has enabled health sharing (`check_health_sharing` ‚Üí global `privacy_settings.health_sharing_default` or the per-team override, surfaced in Settings ‚Üí Privacy ‚Üí "Share health & blood test results with staff"); otherwise `shapeBloodwork` returns a `consent_required` state and the report shows a "hasn't shared" message. The athlete always sees their own. **Wearable** rows already self-filter to `consent_state='granted'`. Load / wellness / physio restrictions still ride the full lens without a separate gate (clinical duty-of-care, owner directive) ‚Äî if those should also become athlete-gated, add the check in `monitoring-report.js` (helpers exist in `consent-guard.js`). The in-code comment at `resolveRequesterRole` + `shapeBloodwork` documents this.
- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî "finish the app" Option 1: safe-polish dedup + QB form gap closed.** (1) **Shared `app-topbar`** ‚Äî the notifications-bell + profile-avatar header was copy-pasted into 11 athlete screens (today/training/wellness/stats/acwr/supplements/achievements/chat/more/competition/gameday). Extracted one `app-topbar` (fixed right side; left eyebrow/title projected via `<ng-content>`). Host is `display:contents` so the wrapper generates no box ‚Äî critical because `.topbar` is `position:sticky/top:0` and a real wrapper box would become its sticky containing block (header-height) and un-pin it; contents keeps the header a layout child of the screen root, renders pixel-identically (Mobile-Responsive visual snapshots stayed green). Dropped 11 now-unused `AvatarComponent` imports + 4 unused `RouterLink` imports. Commits `84c4572c`+`c65b17fe`. (2) **QB throw-count / arm-care form** (Ledger row): the `qb_throwing_sessions` table + `qb-throwing.js` endpoint + `QbThrowingService` already stored/accepted the full session shape, but the quick-log card only collected type/total/after-feeling. Added the missing inputs ‚Äî optional short/medium/long distance split (omitted when 0/0/0 so it never contradicts the total; a soft "breakdown sums to X ¬∑ total is Y" hint), arm-feeling _before_, and the three arm-care compliance toggles (warm-up done / arm-care done / iced) using the app's `.sw` switch pattern; per-session fields reset after a successful log so compliance flags never carry silently into the next session. Pure frontend ‚Äî no migration. Only remaining QB gap is the `QB_THROW_ADAPTATION` interception-rate data source (throw count ‚âÝ interception rate).
- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî "finish the app" Option 2 (first feature): daily-load calendar heatmap.** New self-only endpoint `daily-load.js` (`/api/daily-load`) returns the caller's own session-RPE AU by calendar day over a 35-day window, summed via the **canonical** `computeSessionLoad` (utils/acwr.js) ‚Äî the AU formula is never re-derived (¬ß4). New presentational `app-load-calendar` (Monday-first week grid, colour ramp **relative** to the window max so it makes no absolute AU‚Üírisk claim; raw AU on each cell's tooltip) fed by a testable pure `buildLoadCalendar` util; wired into Stats under a "Daily load" section (skeleton ‚Üí grid ‚Üí honest empty state). Answers "when did load concentrate / when did the ACWR spike," not just today's ratio. Chosen as the safe autonomous Option-2 pick (own data, no consent/health-exposure or safety-formula decision). Tests: 4 backend (`aggregateDailyLoad`) + 4 frontend (`buildLoadCalendar`). **v1 caveat:** training-session load only ‚Äî past-game estimated load (which ACWR folds in) not yet included; the coach/other-athlete view is deferred to the consent path (see the monitoring finding above).
- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî empty/sparse-data + error-state resilience audit (clean, no fix).** Swept for the "renders broken on thin data" class (live DB has ~2 athletes / 1 team). **The app is genuinely well-defended:** (1) **no stuck-loading** ‚Äî data services pipe `catchError(() => of(fallback))`, so a component's next-only `.subscribe()` still resolves its `loading`/`null` signal on error (verified on monitoring-report + the general pattern); (2) **no divide-by-empty NaN** ‚Äî every mean/avg (sleep-debt, wellness-analytics, precision.utils, ai.service insights, monitoring sparkline) guards `length === 0`/`< 2` before dividing; (3) **template interpolations null-guarded** (`x != null ? x.toFixed() : "‚Äî"`; `!` assertions sit inside `@if` guards; progress-bar denominators are constants, never 0); (4) **list screens have honest empty states** (gameday/competition/notifications/achievements all branch on empty); (5) **layout is properly responsive** ‚Äî `.app-shell` is a centered 480px‚Üí720px‚Üí1280px container with a real desktop sidebar grid at ‚â•1024px, not a stretched mobile view. Conclusion: the live "looks broken" reports were specific (exercise-card `align-items`, Merlin consent-gating, a missing coach feature ‚Äî all fixed), not a systemic resilience gap. Remaining polish is visual/UX and best caught by viewing the rendered app.

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî per-persona end-to-end flow audit (all 5 roles, no new bugs).** Traced each persona's full journey to catch the "green CI, broken for one role" class the coach-routing bug came from. **Result: the staff flows are well-built and correctly gated ‚Äî the routing bug was the ONLY thing breaking them** (all staff were landing on the athlete app; fixed earlier today). Verified end-to-end: (1) landing routes by team_members role (staff ‚Üí /staff); (2) roster works for ALL lanes ‚Äî coaches fetch `/api/coach/team`, non-coach staff fetch `/api/roster/players` (which resolves the team via `getUserTeamId`, any-membership, not coach-only) ‚Äî an earlier read that looked like "physio roster stuck loading" was a mis-read: the constructor's `else` branch handles non-coach lanes; (3) `athlete-detail` is genuinely role-shaped (physio‚Üí"Injuries & RTP" via `/api/staff-physiotherapist/athletes/:id`, nutrition‚Üí"Body composition", psych‚Üí"Mental wellness"), all three endpoints exist with base+wildcard redirects; (4) cross-lane boundaries hold ‚Äî `staff-nutritionist`/`staff-psychology`/`staff-physiotherapist` each gate to their own role set (`NUTRITION_ACCESS_ROLES` etc.), which don't cross. Non-coach staff nav is intentionally minimal (Roster + More; the squad-monitoring link was added to staff More earlier today). Player persona is the well-trodden path (Merlin/exercises/dividers fixes already shipped). Net: no persona-flow fix needed ‚Äî the earlier routing fix unlocked an already-functional staff experience.

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî write-path / privilege-escalation RLS audit (live, all clear).** Companion to the read-side RLS audit: simulated authenticated non-service-role users attempting destructive writes (rolled-back transactions). **No holes found ‚Äî defense-in-depth throughout.** (1) **Role escalation blocked:** a player setting their own `team_members.role`‚Üícoach is stopped by the `ff_guard_self_team_member_update` trigger (comprehensively guards role/status/team*id/user_id/approval fields on self-update) ‚Äî the UPDATE RLS policy alone is permissive, but role-immutability requires OLD-vs-NEW comparison which only a trigger can do, so this is the correct layering, not a gap; a player INSERTing a coach row is blocked by the INSERT `with_check` (self-insert forced to role='player'). (2) **Cross-user writes blocked:** player UPDATE/DELETE of another athlete's wellness/sessions/readiness ‚Üí 0 rows. (3) **Consent tamper-proof:** a coach cannot edit a player's `privacy_settings` (row present, 0 affected) nor forge `athlete_consent_settings`/`team_sharing_settings` (RLS `with_check` violation) ‚Äî so the performance-sharing consent the training_sessions fix depends on can't be self-granted. (4) **Append-only integrity:** authenticated DELETE on `execution_logs` ‚Üí 0 (RLS + `prevent_execution_log*\*`triggers;`state_transition_history` likewise guarded). Net across both RLS audits: reads and writes are soundly gated; the only real finding was the training_sessions read bypass (fixed, migration 20260709154006).

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî live RLS / direct-data-access security audit.** Simulated authenticated non-service-role users (`SET ROLE authenticated` + JWT claims) against the live DB to prove the RLS backstop actually blocks cross-user reads (backend fns use `supabaseAdmin`/service-role, which BYPASSES RLS ‚Äî so RLS is the last line for direct `/rest/v1` access). **Clean:** no table has RLS off; the only `USING(true)` SELECT policies are on reference/catalog tables (exercise progressions, meal templates, warmup protocols‚Ä¶); all 7 views run `security_invoker=true` (incl. the once-vulnerable `physical_measurements_latest`); a **player** sees only their own wellness/readiness/injuries (0 rows of another user's, even `SELECT *`); a **coach** is blocked from reading players' wellness/readiness/injuries directly (RLS returns 0 ‚Äî the app reaches them via service-role + consent). **FINDING A (FIXED live 2026-07-09, migration `20260709154006`):** the base `training_sessions` SELECT policy let any active team coach read ALL columns (rpe, workload, performance_score, notes‚Ä¶) of team players with NO `check_performance_sharing` consent check, bypassing `v_training_sessions_consent`. **Fixed** by adding `check_performance_sharing(user_id, team_id)` to the coach SELECT branch (own-data branch unchanged; UPDATE/coaching branch intentionally left ‚Äî coaching ‚âÝ viewing shared data). Since all app coach-reads use service-role (which bypasses RLS) or the athlete's own token, and both frontend base-table reads are own-data, this only closes the **direct-REST** hole with zero app impact. Verified live (rolled-back sims): coach still sees a consenting player (14 rows), a non-consenting player ‚Üí 0; athlete own-data intact (14), athlete other-user ‚Üí 0. **Fixed ‚Äî FINDING B:** the squad-monitoring endpoint (`team-monitoring.js`, shipped 2026-07-09) gated its RPE/sRPE/ACWR **load** columns on wellness consent (`canCoachViewWellness`) ‚Äî wrong consent; an athlete could share wellness but not performance and still have load shown. Now split: wellness/readiness under `canCoachViewWellness`, load/ACWR under the new `canCoachViewPerformance` (reuses the DB `can_view_player_performance` ‚Äî same gate as the consent view); ungated columns return null.

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî systemic "source-of-truth mismatch" audit (triggered by the coach-routing bug).** After the root bug (login routing read role from `user.role`/`user_metadata` instead of `team_members`, so every coach/physio landed on the athlete app), swept the same bug-class app-wide. **Route integrity:** all nav targets resolve (only legacy-route ref is a test fixture). **API wiring:** every _called_ frontend `/api/X` has a redirect + function ‚Äî no live 404s; 4 `API_ENDPOINTS` entries are dead defs (`fixtures`, `season.archive`, `player.notifyInactive`, `tournaments.*` ‚Äî never called; `season.archive`'s "routes via team dispatcher" comment is wrong, but moot since unused). **Consent defaults:** fail-closed (no sharing/consent check defaults to allowed). **Authz source:** the 48 role-gated functions all resolve role from `team_members` via `getUserRole` ‚Äî correct; the `user_metadata.role || "player"` reads in `auth-me`/`auth-login`/`auth-helper` are informational (returned to client), never gate authz. **Fixed:** (1) removed the dead `HomeRouteService.getHomeRouteForUser` (still carried the `user.role ?? user_metadata.role` trap for the next dev); only the authoritative `getHomeRouteForRole(role)` remains. (2) **Reachability gap I created:** coaches now land on `/staff`, but the squad-monitoring table was only linked from `/reports` (athlete shell) ‚Äî added it to the staff More menu so staff can actually reach it. (3) Flagged `auth-helper`'s `role` field NOT-AUTHORITATIVE in-code so it isn't reused for gating. **Guards** (auth/staff/homeRedirect) verified correct.

- **2026-07-09 ‚Äî live-app fixes + squad monitoring (from field feedback).** Four issues off the live site: (1) **Squad monitoring** ‚Äî NEW coach/physio squad-wide daily table (`/team-monitoring`, `team-monitoring.js` ‚Üí `/api/team-monitoring`, linked from `/reports`). One row per active team_members athlete: pre-session wellness (sleep/energy/stress/soreness/readiness), internal load (RPE/min/sRPE), ACWR, readiness flags + action, and a MEAN row. **TWO gates, both reusing vetted code:** caller must hold a `HEALTH_DATA_ACCESS_ROLES` role (`getUserRole`), and each athlete row goes through `canCoachViewWellness` (the SAME per-athlete consent check as the single-athlete report) ‚Äî no consent ‚Üí name only, no health data. Live data is currently thin (1 team, 5 team_members, wellness on 2 athletes; `session_load`/`external_load_metrics` empty so no Catapult external-load columns ‚Äî those appear if/when wearable data lands). 6 backend tests on the flag/mean logic. (2) **Merlin** was consent-gated with the only enable path buried in Settings ‚Üí added an inline "Enable Merlin" button in the gated chat state (explicit consent via `updateAiProcessing(true)`, records the consent date). (3) **Today's Exercises** 1-exercise blocks stretched into giant empty cards (`align-items: start`) + dividers between exercises within a block. (4) Readiness/ACWR charts got the data-viz-audit a11y treatment earlier the same window.

- **2026-07-08 ‚Äî reuse/duplication audit (jscpd-measured).** Whole-codebase duplication measured at **3.74%** (3,954 dup lines / 314 clones, tests excluded) ‚Äî already low/healthy (industry "green" is <5%). Breakdown: ~41% is same-file internal repetition (deduping = intra-file abstraction, usually hurts readability ‚Üí left); a large chunk is per-function Netlify router scaffold (OPTIONS/CORS/URL-parse preambles across ~15‚Äì20 entry files ‚Äî inherent to the serverless entry-point pattern ‚Üí left, not worth `createRouter()` indirection touching every endpoint's request path); the rest is coincidental structural similarity (similar query shapes, not shared logic ‚Üí left). **Cleaned the genuine cross-file logic dupes only** (each verbatim-verified + tested): R1 `getTimeAgo` (supabase-client's private copy ‚Üí the canonical `date-utils`); R2 `parseIso`/`validationError`/`str` (three event-write handlers ‚Äî athlete-events/event-travel/event-games each carried copies ‚Üí `utils/event-fields.js`); R3 `mapKnownHandlerError` (the invalid_json/integer-between catch chain ‚Üí `utils/handler-errors.js`, 2 byte-identical exemplars migrated, ~23 structurally-varied ones left to adopt incrementally). **Deliberately left** (genuine but not worth the risk/reward): the profile-edit ‚Üî settings avatar-upload logic (~15L, touches 2 UI components); isometrics/plyometrics (only 2 callers and they return _different_ response shapes ‚Äî nested vs flat ‚Äî so a factory would mask a real API-contract difference); the invitation-validity gate (validate-invitation has a DB side-effect accept-invitation lacks). **The one large remaining lever stays incremental:** ~98 backend functions still do ad-hoc validation vs the shared `validateInput` DSL (F7) ‚Äî a campaign, not a batch, since each has a subtly different accept/reject contract.
- **2026-07-08 ‚Äî codebase consistency audit (propagate the past-day patterns).** Swept the codebase for where the day's single-source/security patterns weren't yet applied. **Fixed:** (C1, safety) injury grade‚Üíseverity-tier classification was triplicated ‚Äî `normalizeSeverity` (F8 canonical), `active-injuries.js injuriesPainLevel` (own rank map), `calc-readiness.js` (inline `=== "severe" || "Grade 3"`); both consumers now route through `normalizeSeverity` (behavior-identical, drives RTP sizing + readiness penalty). (D1, correctness) `wellness.service.ts` typed the check-in `id` as `number` and mapped it via `Number()` ‚Üí `NaN` for the actual `uuid`, silently breaking realtime UPDATE/DELETE matching; id ‚Üí `string`. (B1) `resolveAgeYears` was the last stray re-implementation of `ageFromDob`'s month/day calc (F2); now delegates, keeping only its 16‚Äì80 CNS bound. (E1) `app.constants.ts TRAINING.ACWR_*` duplicated the evidence-preset ACWR thresholds (F9 canonical) but is used only for an ordering sanity-check, not live classification ‚Äî added a drift-guard test locking the two together + a pointer comment. **Investigated, found NOT a bug (no change):** `trends.js`'s `athleteId` interpolated into a `.or()` is fully gated by the handler's `athleteId !== userId ‚Üí 403` check before the query, so it can only ever be the caller's own trusted uuid (contrast the genuinely-exploitable free-text `knowledge-search` case, which WAS fixed). Other `.or()` sites interpolate server-resolved uuids/dates. No other `Number(uuid)` realtime bugs exist (channel/toast services use string ids).
- **2026-07-08 ‚Äî wknw01 security extraction (surgical, not a branch merge).** The `claude/code-architecture-audit-wknw01` branch (14 commits, diverged 2026-06-29, ‚àí34k lines vs a pre-consolidation base) was **never merged** ‚Äî a bulk merge would clobber the past week's consolidation + the F1‚ÄìF8 audit work. Instead each of its security/data-loss findings was triaged against **current main + live DB** and only genuinely-missing fixes re-applied. **Already salvaged on main (verified, no action needed):** ai-chat youth-safety enforcement, the cross-user singleton-leak sign-out guard, password-recovery intent, `platform.service.ts` real `localStorage`, `calc-readiness` null-ACWR guard, `daily-protocol-rtp` injured-region filtering. **Live DB already correct (verified via Supabase MCP + `get_advisors`: 0 security-critical findings ‚Äî no `rls_enabled_no_policy`/`security_definer_view`/`public_bucket`/`function_search_path_mutable`):** `physical_measurements_latest` has `security_invoker=true`, `proactive_checkins`/`team_activities` RLS policies present, community-media broad list policy dropped, anon/public execute revoked on `upsert_wellness_checkin`, `increment_training_points` locked to service_role. **Extracted (genuinely missing):** (1) `knowledge-search.js` interpolated raw `query` into a PostgREST `.or()` filter with no metacharacter sanitization ‚Äî a live injection vector (the H3 hardening fixed the identical pattern in `smart-ai-service.js` but was never applied here); fixed + 3 tests. (2) Six DB security migration **files** (`20260701171132`/`171312`/`234056`/`20260702104954`/`20260703082528`/`082611`) that were applied **live** but never committed to version control ‚Äî backfilled verbatim (each verified to match live state) so a fresh `db reset` reproduces the fixed state instead of re-opening the holes. **Remaining WARN-level advisories (pre-existing, not this pass):** 69√ó `authenticated_security_definer_function_executable` (the accepted SECURITY DEFINER RPC pattern) + 1√ó `auth_leaked_password_protection` (an Auth-dashboard toggle, not code). **Correctness bug found, deferred to the consistency audit:** `wellness.service.ts` coerces the check-in `uuid` id via `Number()` ‚Üí `NaN`, silently breaking realtime UPDATE/DELETE matching (`NaN !== NaN`).
- **2026-07-08 ‚Äî full rules/single-source-of-truth audit.** `docs/generated/live-schema.snapshot.json` had drifted (last captured 2026-07-06, before the entire monitoring-feature migration set + the audit-fix migrations) ‚Äî `docs-regen` was silently working off a stale snapshot showing 169 tables and 11 "unknown table ref" warnings for live tables it didn't know about. **Fixed:** re-captured the snapshot from live (`information_schema` via Supabase MCP) and regenerated `supabase-types.ts` ‚Äî now 181 tables, 0 unknown refs, 0 stale-in-types. Also found and fixed: (1) this doc's own header/¬ß1/¬ß2/¬ß3 stats were stale (188‚Üí181 tables, Angular 21‚Üí22, 224‚Üí249 migration files, 113‚Üí120 functions); (2) the entire **Athlete monitoring** feature (12 migrations, 5 endpoints, the `monitoring-report` screen, shipped 2026-07-07) was never added to ¬ß4 ‚Äî a direct ¬ß8 violation, now added; (3) ¬ß7's auth-hardening note described the OLD vulnerable state (anon-executable SECURITY DEFINER fns) as required/permanent ‚Äî that was fixed live 2026-07-07/08 (revoked, verified 0 anon-executable), but the doc still told a future reader those fns "must stay executable," which would have meant re-introducing the hole if followed; corrected. (4) Law #6 ("server is canonical for the engine") directly contradicted ¬ß5a ("prescription is owned by the client") ‚Äî not a new bug, but the doc asserted both without reconciling them; ¬ß5a now states the honest current split and points at the in-flight migration (¬ß5a note, updated live as that migration progresses).
- **Calculation-ownership audit (2026-07-08, see `docs/ground-truth/calculation-ownership-audit-2026-07-08.md`).** Full per-metric sweep (readiness, ACWR, periodization, RPE/workload, Hooper, weight, injury flags, CNS classification). Found and fixed: a dead ACWR spike-cap service that wrote to a table dropped months earlier (illusory safety feature, deleted); two divergent high-CNS session classifiers (unified onto one, now incl. `competition`); a hand-copied ACWR risk-zone block in `load-management.js` (now reads `ACWR_RISK_ZONES`); an inconsistent inline workload formula across 3 log paths (now `utils/session-load.js`). **Still open, deliberately not auto-fixed:** `wellness-checkin.js`'s check-in-time readiness estimate and `calc-readiness.js`'s composite readiness are two distinct, independently-tuned formulas (not a mechanical duplicate ‚Äî the check-in formula has a deliberate scale-inversion safeguard the composite lacks); true unification is a sports-science decision needing a before/after delta, not something to merge blindly. The canonical wellness-index scorer was extracted to `utils/readiness-score.js` as the safe partial fix.

- **2026-07-07 ‚Äî UI/UX audit close-out + doc consolidation.** Actioned the safe, bounded items from the retired `UIUX_MODERNIZATION_AUDIT.md`: removed 3 zero-reference production deps (`chart.js`/`html2canvas`/`jspdf`); made the Stats "Performance" section an honest "will appear" state (no CTA the athlete can't act on ‚Äî combine-test logging isn't built); made the Today readiness card fully tappable to `/wellness`; added a short-viewport (`max-height: 700px`) collapse of the decorative `.screen-hero` images so content leads on landscape phones. The two one-off analysis docs (`UIUX_MODERNIZATION_AUDIT.md`, `V2_PROPOSAL.md` ‚Äî the latter's V2.0‚ÄìV2.4 flagship already shipped) were **deleted and consolidated into `docs/BACKLOG.md`** (a slim living list of un-built work + the "don't build" guidance). Ground truth stays here; the backlog is only not-yet-built.

- **2026-07-07 ‚Äî Angular-22 code-audit fixes** (the audit doc itself was deleted once actioned; this is the durable record). The Angular app audited clean overall (44/44 OnPush, fully `@if/@for` + signals + `inject()`, ~zero `any`); these were the actionable findings:
  - **Systemic cross-user-leak guard (root-singleton state):** `SupabaseService.signOut()` now does a full `window.location.assign("/login")` after clearing the session + SW cache ‚Äî a reload resets every root singleton's in-memory state by construction, so no user-scoped service can serve a previous user's cached role/injuries/settings to the next sign-in on a shared device. This is the systemic backstop for the whole leak class; the per-service `userId`-keyed guards on `team-membership`/`periodization` (2026-07-07 above) remain as defense-in-depth.
  - **Regression tests for the shipped security fixes:** new specs lock `team-membership.service` (cross-user cache isolation), `platform.service` (real localStorage/sessionStorage round-trip), and `auth-flow-data.service` (password-recovery intent set‚Üídetect‚Üíexpire). +9 tests (Angular suite 461 ‚Üí 470).
  - **Shared typed event helper:** `shared/utils/event.utils.ts` (`eventValue`/`eventNumber`/`eventChecked`) replaces the `val(e)`/`num(e)` helpers that were copy-pasted into 3 components. Inline `$any($event.target)` in templates left as-is (idiomatic, low-value churn).
  - **`standalone: true` removed** from all 44 components (redundant default since Angular v19).
  - **Design-token inline violations:** of 303 inline `style="‚Ä¶"`, only **3** used hardcoded non-token values (`gap:4px` √ó3 ‚Üí `var(--s-1)`); fixed. The other ~300 are token-compliant one-liners ‚Äî deliberately **not** migrated to SCSS (CSS-specificity churn with real visual-regression risk, unverifiable without the Linux-baselined visual suite, for ~zero benefit since they already use tokens).
  - **Deferred (not done, logged):** decompose the large services (`periodization` 2.6k / `channel` 1.3k / `acwr` 1.3k ‚Äî periodization is well-sectioned + 94 tests, split is behavior-neutral-but-optional; `channel.service` is untested and the more urgent one); backfill tests for `channel.service`/`privacy-settings.service`; adopt `httpResource()` on read paths incrementally.

- **2026-07-07 ‚Äî security & data-loss fixes salvaged from the stale audit branch** (`origin/claude/code-architecture-audit-wknw01`, code-only ‚Äî no live-DB migration). That branch (14 commits, forked from the pre-session June-29 main, a ‚àí34k-line dead-code sweep) overlaps heavily with work already merged (v2.4, cleanup, archaudit); a blind merge would resurrect what main deliberately moved past. Instead its still-live fixes were extracted surgically and verified against current main. Its DB/RLS commits were already superseded (the `proactive_checkins`/`team_activities` RLS-gap fix + storage-listing fix landed live via v2.4 as `20260703082528`/`20260703082611`; 8/10 of its migration-reconciliation files are already on main). What was genuinely missing and is now applied:
  - **Cross-user data leak on shared devices (P0)** ‚Äî `team-membership.service.ts` + `periodization.service.ts` are root singletons that outlive in-SPA sign-out (no page reload), so a second user signing in within the 30s cache TTL got the FIRST user's role/team/injuries/settings/travel. Fixed with a `userId`-keyed cache guard (`cached.userId === user.id`, `_loadPromiseUserId`) and a userId-change `effect()` that reloads recent-sessions/injuries/settings/travel and clears them on sign-out. **Injury leak was safety-relevant** (unguarded sprint work for an athlete with a real injury, or a phantom guard for one without).
  - **Password reset permanently broken** ‚Äî `supabase.service.ts`'s `PASSWORD_RECOVERY` handler never called `AuthFlowDataService.markPasswordRecoveryIntent()`, and `platform.service.ts`'s storage helpers were pure in-memory (never touched real `localStorage`/`sessionStorage`), so the recovery intent couldn't survive the reset link's fresh tab. Both fixed.
  - **RTP not filtered by injured region** ‚Äî `daily-protocol-rtp.js` promised "focus on areas NOT injured" but never filtered exercise pools; could prescribe a hamstring stretch to a hamstring injury. Now filters via the exported `isExerciseSafeForInjuries` (rehab block deliberately exempt).
  - **AI youth-safety never enforced on the streaming/SSE path** ‚Äî `ai-chat.js` checked a non-existent `isYouthBlocked` field (always false); now uses `youthRestrictions?.isBlocked` matching the already-fixed non-streaming path (block + parent notification). Request-race guards added to `wellness.service.ts`/`readiness.service.ts`; optimistic-rollback on failed coach-alert ack; gameday hydration total load.
  - **Partial settings save wiped every other field (P0 data-loss, ¬ß5b violation)** ‚Äî `player-settings.js`'s `athlete_training_config` upsert wrote every optional field unconditionally (`value || default`), so saving only position reset season calendar/equipment/etc. to defaults. Fixed with the conditional-spread pattern (`x !== undefined ? {‚Ä¶} : {}`) `teamTrainingDays` already used; age-derived fields only recompute when `birthDate` is in the payload. +2 regression tests. _(From `24c7bdf3`; its bundled season-calendar-region-presets feature was NOT taken ‚Äî it overlaps the archaudit season calendar already on main.)_
  - **Not taken (superseded/divergent):** the branch's dead-code sweep, 7-stage season calendar, 77‚Üí286 exercise-library expansion, and migration renames are already done differently on main. **Still open:** the branch remains unmerged; nothing else from it is planned. Its remaining value is exhausted by the above.

- **2026-07-06 ‚Äî dead-schema cleanup (applied live via Supabase MCP; migrations `20260706185636`, `20260706185652`, `20260706190823`):**
  - **19 dead tables dropped** (all 0 rows, zero references in app code, views, or function bodies; verified by two independent audits): `video_assignments`, `video_bookmarks`, `video_curation_status`, `video_watch_history`, `video_playlists` (shelved video-library family ‚Äî `training_videos` is the live one), `weekly_training_analysis`, `player_game_summary`, `coach_film_sessions`, `coach_film_tags`, `coach_playbook_plays`, `player_development_goals`, `player_development_notes`, `player_skill_assessments`, `ai_feedback` (superseded by live `ai_response_feedback`), `ai_review_queue`, `consent_change_log`, `event_lineups`, `team_preferences`, `coach_athlete_assignments`. Base tables 188 ‚Üí 169. DDL snapshot for the 10 that had no local migration file: `backups/20260706_dropped_dead_tables_ddl.json`.
  - **Dropped-in-error, restored same session:** `event_games` + `athlete_travel_log` were initially dropped on main-only evidence while the Tournament Mode v2.x code was still on its unmerged branch ‚Äî restored verbatim (0 rows, so schema restore = full restore) incl. `event_participation.game_id`. The v2.x branch has since been merged to main (2026-07-06), closing that gap. **Lesson recorded: zero-reference checks must cover unmerged branches, not just main.**
  - **`coach_athlete_assignments` was a dead authorization path:** referenced only inside 3 other tables' RLS policies (`execution_logs`, `readiness_scores`, `session_version_history`) as an OR-branch, but nothing ever inserted a row ‚Äî the branch could never grant access. Policies recreated without it (behavior-neutral); real staff access is the `team_members` path.
  - **10 missing FK constraints added** (all pre-verified: matching types, 0 orphans; `user_id‚Üíusers` uses ON DELETE CASCADE to align with GDPR hard-delete): `event_availability.user_id`, `execution_logs.exercise_id` (SET NULL), `ff_exercise_mappings.exercisedb_exercise_id`, `flag_pull_stats/passing_stats/receiving_stats.game_event_id`, `nutrition_reports.team_id` (SET NULL), `proactive_checkins.user_id`, `readiness_scores.user_id`, `team_activities.team_id`.
  - **Wrong-wired, FK impossible (varchar keys) ‚Äî NOT fixed, needs a deliberate type migration:** the legacy game-stats family stores keys as `character varying` against int/uuid parents: `games.team_id` (varchar vs `teams.id` uuid), `game_events.game_id`/`team_id`, `flag_pull_stats`/`passing_stats`/`receiving_stats.game_id` (varchar vs `games.id` int), `situational_stats.user_id`, `user_notification_preferences.user_id` (varchar vs `users.id` uuid). Code joins these in JS, so nothing errors at runtime ‚Äî but no referential integrity is possible until the columns are retyped. `position_specific_metrics.position_id` has no parent table at all.
  - **Rules tables are curated but unwired:** `taper_rules` (5 rows), `weather_substitution_rules` (8), `contraindication_rules` (22) hold real methodology content, but the engine uses hardcoded constants (`TAPER_CONFIG` etc.) instead of reading them. `prescription_audit_log` (0 rows, same reconstructed feature family) has no writer yet ‚Äî kept. Wiring these is a deliberate engine change (the periodization preserve-list applies), not a mechanical fix.
  - **Migration-file drift:** the 2026-07-06 v2.x merge restored the local migration file set (224 files + this cleanup's 3 = 227). Residual known mismatch: the six v2.x files carry authoring timestamps (`20260702120000`‚Ä¶`20260704100000`) that differ from their applied versions (`20260703024214`‚Ä¶`20260703175554`) ‚Äî same benign class as the 2026-06-25 reconciliation cases.
  - **Over-broad staff RLS observation (pre-existing, unchanged):** the recreated `readiness_scores`/`session_version_history` SELECT policies grant any active `physiotherapist`/`medical_staff`/`admin`/`owner` in ANY team read access to ALL rows (not scoped to shared-team). API-layer consent gating (`coach-core.js` blocked set) is the effective control; tightening RLS to shared-team scope would be defense-in-depth ‚Äî a deliberate change, logged here rather than snuck in.

- **2026-06-10 ‚Äî engine personalization & safety pass (athlete-driven brief):** the prescription engine is now age-, position-, region-, season- and travel-aware. All changes push in the _safe_ direction (more conservative); adversarially verified.
  - **Nutrition**: `CARB_PER_KG` periodised to energy expenditure ‚Äî a short sprint day is 4.5 g/kg (was a flat 6 ‚Üí 480g for 80kg), pre-game/game-day stay elevated (6/7). Heat now adds +0.5L fluid (was documented, never applied).
  - **Injury regions** (`athlete-injuries.js restrictionsFor`): added **soleus** + patella/adductor/tibialis/plantar/ITB/tendon to lower-limb, a **core/trunk** category, and an **upper-body** category; an unrecognised region now **fails safe** (restricts sprint + high-intensity + throwing). The client injury guard derives `restrictsThrowing` so an upper-body issue pulls throwing/snapping without touching running.
  - **Age** (`cnsRecoveryHoursForAge`): CNS recovery window <35‚Üí48h, 35‚Äì39‚Üí60h, 40+‚Üí72h (monotonic, floored, from `date_of_birth`).
  - **Position** (`withPositionEmphasis`): QB/WR-DB/center accessory-prehab emphasis (additive only ‚Äî never changes intent/load), surfaced on Today; throwing-restriction overrides QB/center into a protect-the-arm message.
  - **Tournament congestion**: `peakDayGameCount` now a conservative worst-day estimate (`ceil(games/(days-1))`, not the average ‚Äî averaging fail-open fixed); ‚â•3 games/day trips heavy-density de-load regardless of the 10/14d total.
  - **Season phases**: added **peak** (sharp/low-volume) and **postseason** (regeneration); split seasons (multiple windows incl. a mid-season off gap) resolve per day via `macroPhaseFor`. Onboarding picker offers Off/Pre/In/Peak/Post-season.
  - **Travel**: `daily_wellness_checkin.travel_hours` + extended `upsert_wellness_checkin` RPC + a wellness chip control + a bounded **readiness penalty** in `calc-readiness` (1‚Äì2h ‚àí2, 3‚Äì5h ‚àí4, ‚â•6h ‚àí8; subtract-only).
  - **Knowledge base**: 30 ‚Üí **104** evidence-based, merlin-approved entries (hamstrings, knees, glutes, core, isometrics‚Üíplyometrics, warm-ups, rest, age bands, QB/WR/DB/center demands, travel, nutrition periodisation). Adult-male-16+ only.
  - Calibration constants (carb g/kg, CNS hours, congestion threshold, travel penalty) are documented in-code as **starting points** for the coach to tune against team history.
  - **Per-game ACWR load**: `calc-readiness` now injects an estimated per-game internal load (~350 AU, tunable) for PAST games from the schedule spine into the daily-load map (multi-day events spread across days; per-day MAX(logged, estimate) so it never lowers a day and avoids double-count), so a tournament's acute load/ACWR rises instead of reading falsely safe.
  - ~~**Open / deferred:** QB throw-count / center snap-count ‚Üí quantified load gating (needs the throw/snap logs wired + the coach's dosing thresholds)~~ **Reassessed 2026-06-25**: not actually a "needs building" gap ‚Äî `netlify/functions/qb-throwing.js` (424 lines, arm-care endpoint, writes `training_sessions.throw_au`/`throw_count`) and the `api.service.ts` route table already exist but have **zero callers anywhere in the Angular app** ‚Äî orphaned, same pattern as the ExerciseDB lane in ¬ß4. No UI ever logs a throw count, so there's no data for the engine to gate on regardless of dosing-threshold decisions. Region-specific prehab/return-to-play _progression_ engine (the knowledge base covers the education); season leap-day (Feb 29) + user-declared overlapping windows are minor edge cases. `ensure_public_user_profile` has a pre-existing `last_name` NOT NULL quirk for email-only auth users (affects all wellness writes for such users).

- **2026-06-10 ‚Äî architecture refactor + COMPOSE wiring (two engines clarified):** an audit confirmed `periodization.service.ts` (Angular, **intent/targets** ‚Äî Today/Training/Gameday) and `daily-protocol.js` (backend, **exercise realization**) are TWO engines. Decision: **compose** (intent layer authoritative, daily-protocol realizes exercises for it). Do NOT delete daily-protocol (a 10-module, routed, tested engine; the earlier "dead code" read was wrong).
  - **Class 3 (config extraction, behaviour-neutral):** `TAPER_CONFIG` (taper windows/targets centralised); `baseTargets` (in-season) vs `BUILD_TARGET_OVERRIDES` (pre-season heavier on light intents) ‚Äî resolves audit **M1** (the divergence is deliberate, now explicit); `INJURY_RESPONSE` (injury caps). Each verified byte-identical against the 85-anchor periodization preserve-list.
  - **Class 1 / finding 1.1:** a practice day on a recovery phase is now honoured at recovery intensity (a `recovery` row in `PRACTICE_PHASE_MODIFIERS`) instead of being dropped; intent is data-driven per row.
  - **COMPOSE backend:** `daily-protocol /generate` optionally takes `{intent, intentLabel, position}`; `mapIntentToSession` + `positionFlagsFor` realize exercises for that intent and fix the `isQB`/center bug (raw `'qb'` ‚âÝ `'quarterback'`). **Backward-compatible** ‚Äî no intent ‚Üí legacy path byte-unchanged (8 legacy + 8 mapping tests).
